title: Henckelia humilis
intro: Henckelia humilis, also known as the dwarf Gentian, is a species of flowering plant in the Gentianaceae family. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including its ability to treat fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Henckelia humilis include iridoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Henckelia humilis is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat coughs, colds, and flu, as well as to support digestive health and immune function.
taxonomy: Henckelia humilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This family includes a wide variety of flowering plants commonly known as gesneriads. Henckelia humilis is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Malaysia and Indonesia. Within the Gesneriaceae family, Henckelia humilis belongs to the genus Henckelia, which is a group of flowering plants that are often epiphytes or terrestrial species. Henckelia humilis is further classified into the order Lamiales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants.
